title: Agoseris pterocarpa
intro: Agoseris pterocarpa, also known as the winged agoseris or winged milkweed, is a perennial herbaceous plant that belongs to the sunflower family (Asteraceae). The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, cough, and respiratory infections, due to its anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Agoseris pterocarpa include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and alkaloids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to support respiratory health and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu.
taxonomy: Agoseris pterocarpa is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. This classification places Agoseris pterocarpa within the order Asterales, which consists of flowering plants commonly known as asterids. Further, it is classified as a member of the genus Agoseris, which is a group of annual or perennial plants native to North America. The family Asteraceae, also known as the daisy or sunflower family, is a diverse group of flowering plants. Agoseris pterocarpa's classification reflects its botanical relationships and shared characteristics with other plants within its family and genus.
uses: Agoseris pterocarpa, also known as winged milkvetch or winged desert chicory, has been used in traditional medicine by Native American tribes.
